transcript.clean_text: stage 18 of the jedi italia was the final opportunity for many a certain type of rider to take a stage would it be those attackers who can't get up the mountains or the sprinters the longest stage of the judo took everybody from rovereto all the way down to stradella where they would finish for the first time since maxiandry had been victorious in 1994. mainly flat stage with four short hills at the end to add quite a bit of spice to a long day a really hectic start saw it be around 50 kilometers before we had a breakaway form there were many an attempt but it hadn't really gone until we got into the final 100 and 890 case 23 riders were in it segan and co had to work really hard to stop anybody else from trying to join first intermediate takes place in cremona that was won by johnny vermesh and then the thought was towards the stage win into the last four hills the gap at over 15 minutes patrick bevin launching an early attack that after batistela and zana had both had a go it would be when remy cavania attacked however that the race would really start to kick off the frenchman would pick up almost half a minute of a gap alberto petiole also reacted well nicholas roach was strong too betty all making up half of the second time gap on the penultimate climb roach then getting back to betiol kavanaugh on that last climb with 15 seconds before roach was dropped betty all in beast mode going after the tgv from clermont hall betiola would leave cavanya behind and that would ensure that the koenig quick steps three-year run so far of not winning a stage at the g would continue the locomotive unable to chug on to the finish betiol was on a special day however former winner of the tour of flanders looked like he was riding a classic kavanagh completely out of gas and passed by roach roach's the only man able to challenge petiole it was still 20 seconds to make up a shake of their head inside the last couple of kilometers it was left to betiol to enjoy the moment an ef education nippo up there in the gc with hugh carthy and now stage winners thanks to betio there'd be a superb ride behind from simoniconsomni to finish second and nicholas roach would get his giro best placing of third all the delight for betsyal and the american team ef education nippo betty all now a grand tour stage winner he won by 17 seconds from the advancing group consonant roach aunt and alicia in the top five and stage eight yet again going to the breakaway the break winning by its biggest margin so far over 23 minutes passed when the peloton and the malaya rosa came over the line and that all meant that the mayanosa would stay on the back of aegon bernal going into the final two mountain stages and the time trial 221 his gap over carruzo yates at 323 over six minutes to fourth 609 to karthi in fifth so three days to go in the gyro and a slightly altered stage heading to alpe di mera on stage 19. leaving abiate grazo 10 kilometers long and one less severe climb than we were expected to take on after the awful disaster at moterone just a few days before the stage stage finishing up at alpe di mera another first category climb and yet another uphill finish just two mounting stages remain maximum of 14 percent a tough final 10 kilometers the giro d'italia in play three days to go cane bernal hold on or can somebody challenge him we're not too far from finding out wherever you're watching around the world make sure you tune in the general italia is now at its business end
